one_liner: Pinterest provides a public REST API (v5) with OAuth 2.0 authentication, requiring app registration and manual approval (Trial then Standard access) rather than instant self-serve keys.

auth_methods.notes: OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow with refresh tokens; product-limited (test) tokens available for 24-hour trial testing without full OAuth setup.

self_serve_notes: Developers register an app via My Apps and are granted free Trial access, but must submit a video demo of OAuth + live integration for manual review to reach Standard (production) access. No paid tiers, but approval gating exists.

api_surface.notes: Pinterest API v5 covers Pins, Boards, Ads/Campaigns, Catalogs, Analytics, and more, documented via OpenAPI spec; Python SDK available.

main_blocker: Requires manual app approval (Trial then Standard access review with video demo) before production-level API/OAuth access is granted, which slows self-serve automation.

evidence.supports: OAuth 2.0 flow, app registration, trial access, product-limited tokens, redirect URI setup
Trial vs Standard access tiers and review process
Official quickstart repo with OAuth and API examples, including client credentials support
Full OpenAPI v5 spec indicating broad REST API surface
